sources.name: Central Electricity Authority
sources.authority: Ministry of Power, Government of India

coverage.spatial: 4 of 35 states/UTs (TN, KL, AS, WB) — see notes

indicator.title: Installed power-generation capacity (siting basis)
indicator.description: Nameplate capacity of power plants physically located within each state. NOT a measure of how much electricity each state consumes — much of the capacity feeds the regional grid and serves multiple states under CEA allocations. Read this map as 'where the plants stand', not 'who uses the power'.
indicator.methodology_vintage: OpenStreetMap-derived plant inventory snapshot, community-curated; cross-referenced against CEA broad-strokes only
indicator.notes: v1 limitation: rollup is restricted to states already in datasets/reference/in/states.json with reference data (currently TN, KL, AS, WB). Of 35 states/UTs, only 4 render on this map; the other 31 appear grey because we lack data, not because they have zero capacity. v2 plan (see docs/research/energy-power-plants.md): switch upstream to CEA's monthly Installed Capacity report (PDF tabular extract), which gives state-wise totals for ALL states with proper methodology. Until then, treat this map as illustrative of what the schema can do, not as a credible state-comparison.
